brands.name: Bibico
brands.ethics_summary: Onboarded by the Scout agent from bibico.co.uk's own product feed — every listed item discloses its full fibre composition. Brand ethics profile pending manual review.

brands.name: Lucy & Yak
brands.ethics_summary: Brighton-born independent brand known for organic-cotton dungarees and colourful separates, made in audited factories with published supplier lists.

brands.name: Beaumont Organic
brands.ethics_summary: Manchester-based contemporary-conscious label working in organic cotton and linen, produced in small runs in Portugal with a focus on garment longevity.

brands.name: Komodo
brands.ethics_summary: London label trading since 1988, an early adopter of organic cotton, hemp and TENCEL with Soil Association-certified supply chains.

brands.name: People Tree
brands.ethics_summary: Fair Trade pioneer and long-standing Fair Wear Foundation member, working largely in GOTS-certified organic cotton with artisan producer groups.

brands.name: Uniqlo
brands.ethics_summary: Japanese everyday-basics retailer. Runs the RE.UNIQLO garment take-back and down-recycling schemes, though scale and synthetic use remain challenges.

brands.name: Seasalt Cornwall
brands.ethics_summary: Cornish clothing brand and an early adopter of organic cotton (Soil Association certified), focused on durable everyday pieces.

brands.name: M&S
brands.ethics_summary: UK high-street retailer. Its long-running 'Plan A' programme targets recycled and responsibly-sourced materials across a very broad range.

brands.name: Thought
brands.ethics_summary: UK brand built around natural, lower-impact fibres — organic cotton, hemp, bamboo and TENCEL — with a considered slow-fashion positioning.

brands.name: Patagonia
brands.ethics_summary: Outdoor brand and certified B Corp; an industry leader on recycled materials, repairs (Worn Wear), fair labour and environmental activism.

brands.name: Zara
brands.ethics_summary: Inditex's flagship fast-fashion label. Its 'Join Life' line uses some preferred materials, but high volume and rapid turnover raise real overproduction concerns.

brands.name: Sweaty Betty
brands.ethics_summary: UK activewear brand increasingly using recycled polyester and nylon, though performance synthetics still dominate the range.

brands.name: H&M
brands.ethics_summary: Global fast-fashion retailer. Runs 'Conscious' lines and in-store recycling, but has faced scrutiny over greenwashing and sheer production volume.

brands.name: & Other Stories
brands.ethics_summary: H&M-group brand with a design-led range that includes some responsibly-sourced and recycled materials.

brands.name: Finisterre
brands.ethics_summary: Cornish cold-water and outdoor brand, a certified B Corp focused on recycled, traceable materials, PFC-free finishes and repairs.

brands.name: COS
brands.ethics_summary: H&M-group label known for minimal, longer-lasting design; incorporates some recycled and responsibly-sourced fabrics.
